epic.title: Integrations: Xero & calendar
epic.summary: Outbound integrations that remove double-entry: push sales/payments to Xero, and keep appointments in sync with staff calendars (M365 / Google) — each behind a swappable adapter, with an AU/APP-8 sub-processor posture.

stories.title: Xero invoice/payment sync
stories.context: On checkout, create/sync invoice + payment (and payouts) in Xero with account/GST mapping, retries and reconciliation status, behind IAccountingExport (REQ-INT-1).
stories.i_want: completed sales/payments to post to Xero with the right account and GST
acceptance: A completed sale creates the corresponding Xero invoice + payment with correct account + GST.
Failures retry and show a reconciliation status.
Services vs retail vs memberships map to the right accounts (defaults are an open question).
detail: On checkout, create/sync the invoice + payment (and payouts) in Xero with correct account + GST mapping, retries and a reconciliation status — behind an IAccountingExport port. Services vs retail vs memberships map to the right accounts (defaults an open question).
Removes double-entry so the books reconcile without re-keying; the app keeps pricing/reporting while the ledger lives in Xero (ADR-0027).
ui_spec: Prototype: Settings -> Integrations (settings-integrations.png) — Xero connection card + account/GST mapping + sync/reconciliation status; per-sale posting is automatic from Checkout.

data_model.fields: id, provider, ref(invoice/payment), status, retries, reconciled(bool)

stories.title: Two-way calendar sync (M365 / Google)
stories.context: Appointments sync both ways with Outlook/Google; external busy events block availability, behind ICalendarProvider (REQ-INT-2).
stories.i_want: my appointments to appear in Outlook/Google and external busy-time to block my availability
stories.so_that: I have one source of truth for my time
acceptance: Creating/moving/cancelling an appointment reflects in the linked Outlook/Google calendar and vice-versa.
External busy events block availability in booking (PRD-02).
Per-staff opt-in and conflict-resolution rules (open question) supported.
detail: Two-way sync of appointments with Outlook (MS Graph) and Google Calendar behind an ICalendarProvider port: creating/moving/cancelling reflects both ways, and external busy events block availability in booking (PRD-02). Per-staff opt-in and conflict-resolution rules (open question).
Gives practitioners one source of truth for their time.
ui_spec: Prototype: Settings -> Integrations (settings-integrations.png) — per-staff calendar connect (M365/Google) + two-way sync toggle; external busy time shows as unavailable in Schedule.

data_model.fields: id, staff_id, provider(m365|google), tokens, sync_mode(two_way), status
id, calendar_link_id, appointment_id, direction, at, result

stories.title: Sub-processor residency posture (APP-8)
stories.context: No integration sends PII to a non-AU sub-processor unless an APP-8 assessment + consent record exists (REQ-INT-3, C21/ADR-0016).
stories.i_want: assurance that any data leaving the platform goes only to AU-resident or APP-8-assessed sub-processors
acceptance: No integration sends PII to a non-AU sub-processor unless an APP-8 assessment + consent record exists.
All integrations are outbound and swappable.
detail: No integration sends PII to a non-AU sub-processor unless an APP-8 assessment + consent record exists; flows are registered, and all integrations are outbound and swappable (ADR-0012/0016). Ties into PRD-01/RESIDENCY enforcement.
Keeps integrations inside cross-border privacy rules (C21).
ui_spec: Prototype: Settings -> Integrations (settings-integrations.png) — each integration shows its data-residency posture; an admin sub-processor register lists flows + APP-8 status.

data_model.fields: (shared with PRD-01) id, name, region, app8_assessment_ref, consent_ref

stories.title: Online checkout, e-prescribing, webhooks/API (placeholder)
stories.context: Online checkout & deposits (S4 never priced/sold online), e-prescribing (eRx/ETP, 🔬), public API/webhooks (Phase 3) and Medicare/HICAPS (non-applicable to cosmetic). Placeholder (REQ-INT-2a/4/5/6/7, ADR-0035/0036).
stories.i_want: future integrations: online checkout/deposits, e-prescribing and a public API
stories.so_that: the platform extends as we scale
acceptance: Placeholder — Phase 2/3; each behind its existing port (IPaymentProvider, IPrescribingProvider).
S4 is never priced or sold online (invariant carried forward).
Medicare/HICAPS recorded as non-applicable to cosmetic.
detail: Placeholder (Phase 2/3): online checkout & deposits (S4 never priced/sold online), e-prescribing (eRx/ETP, needs validation), public API/webhooks (Phase 3), and Medicare/HICAPS (recorded as non-applicable to cosmetic). Each sits behind its existing port (IPaymentProvider, IPrescribingProvider).
Captured so the architecture stays extension-ready (ADR-0035/0036).
ui_spec: Prototype: Settings -> Integrations (settings-integrations.png) shows these as concept cards (mostly disabled).

data_model.notes: Behind existing ports; S4 never online.
